name: g15_transport_host_state_machine
purpose: Host-scale lifted transport law extending the G30 transport state into a full G60 chronology.
core_claim: G60 is the host chronology of the lifted transport law. It does not replace G15 or G30, but orders them into a 60-step cycle.

phase.meaning: subjective/objective style toggle carried through the lift

host_pass.meaning: which 15-step quarter of the 60-step host chronology is active

G60_to_G30.statement: Forgetting host_pass projects the host chronology to the lifted G30 state.

G30_to_G15.statement: Forgetting sheet projects the lifted state to the G15 shadow.

step_45.name: third_pass_return
step_45.statement: Third-pass host return mirrors the sign-flipped closure at the higher host level.

host_pass_semantics.1: sign-flipped quarter after first G15 closure
host_pass_semantics.2: identity-restored quarter after G30 midpoint closure
host_pass_semantics.3: final return quarter before full host closure

shell_render_reading.statement: A G60-aware shell page should show the lifted state as a host-ordered chronology, not as an edge-coverage search.

default_lens_guidance.tetra: best default for host-scale projected state
default_lens_guidance.cube: best teaching lens for stairs-centered interpretation
default_lens_guidance.sheet: best analysis lens for stacked transport regions

progression_rules.from_G15_to_G30: Retain sheet memory instead of forgetting it.
progression_rules.from_G30_to_G60: Retain host_pass chronology instead of collapsing repeated traversals into the same lifted state.
progression_rules.anti_pattern: Do not attempt to derive G60 by repeatedly drawing more G15 shadow edges.

anti_drift_rules: Do not treat G60 as a new local walk law unrelated to G15 and G30.
Do not forget that G30 is identity-restoring at step 30.
Do not model G60 without an explicit host_pass or equivalent chronology coordinate.
Do not confuse host chronology with sheet memory.
Do not let edge-coverage heuristics override the host closure law.
implementation_priority: Add host_pass to the shell-page state readout.
Render G15 as sign closure, G30 as identity closure, and G60 as host closure.
Keep the signed-lift lens separate from the host-chronology lens.
Use this file as the source of truth for any future G60 shell page.
